root_cause: Dataset source divergence: extract_hidden_states.py loaded from full MATH (~5000 problems via hendrycks/competition_math or EleutherAI/hendrycks_math), while extract_attention_weights.py and evaluate_correctness.py fell through to HuggingFaceH4/MATH-500 (500 problems) because hendrycks/competition_math and lighteval/MATH became unavailable on HuggingFace between extraction sessions.

math500_hidden_states.npz.source: full MATH (~5000 problems, likely hendrycks/competition_math or EleutherAI/hendrycks_math)

attention_ph_diagrams.npz.evidence: 25/level with min(25, pool) where MATH-500 L1 has 43 >= 25

math500_correctness.npz.evidence: 43 L1 = min(100, 43), 90 L2 = min(100, 90) — matches MATH-500 pool sizes

d10_overlap_count: indeterminate (different datasets, estimated 2-7 out of 25 per level by pool-size simulation)
d10_mismatch_type: cross-dataset (full MATH ~5000 vs MATH-500), not just seed-offset

d10_shuffle_test.description: Within-level random pairing produces indistinguishable binding scores from the observed (mismatched) pairing. Both show perfect monotonic decrease (Spearman r=-1.0).
d10_shuffle_test.conclusion: The difficulty trend is a POPULATION-LEVEL signal reflecting per-level topological homogeneity, NOT per-problem attention-hidden coupling. The mismatch does not destroy the trend because the trend does not depend on matching specific problems.
d10_shuffle_test.interpretation_change: OLD: 'attention-hidden coupling decreases with difficulty'. NEW: 'topological similarity between attention and hidden-state spaces decreases with difficulty (population-level)'.

d10_new_scores.note: From attention_binding_results_aligned.json. Trend preserved (r=-0.9, p=0.037). L1 is HIGHER with proper matching (0.740 vs 0.683), confirming per-problem coupling adds signal. Non-monotonic at L2/L3 (L3>L2), breaking the perfect r=-1.0 from mismatched data.

d2_mismatch_type: cross-dataset (hidden states from full MATH, correctness labels from MATH-500)
d2_note: AUROC jumped from 0.580 (mismatched) to 0.787 (aligned). The mismatch was suppressing a real and substantial predictive signal. Top feature: H0_persistence_entropy (0.824). Per-level: L1=0.757, L2=0.691, L3=0.742, L4=0.667. Class balance is extreme (38/433 = 8.8% correct).

d6_details.d6_note: Level 1 minimum universal (4/4), non-monotonicity universal (4/4). Accurate claim: 'Level 1 has lowest H1 persistence entropy across all 4 architectures'. Phi2 is nearly monotonically increasing (Spearman r=0.9, p=0.037).

d6_cross_model_dataset_issue.description: Qwen was extracted from full MATH, other 3 models from MATH-500. Cross-model comparisons are on different problem sets, but the H1 entropy metric is computed per-level aggregate, not per-problem, so the finding is likely robust.
d6_cross_model_dataset_issue.mitigation: Re-extract Qwen from MATH-500 (scripts/extract_hidden_states_aligned.py) for strict alignment.

stablelm_level1_h1_note: Only 1 finite H1 feature with persistence 2.06. Entropy of a single element is 0 (mathematically correct). Level 1 StableLM representations form a near-convex cloud with essentially no loop structure. Level 5 comparison: 12 H1 features, entropy=2.23.
safe_directions_note: D1, D3, D5, D7, D8, D9 all use only the Qwen hidden states (math500_hidden_states.npz) internally. No cross-file alignment needed. Results are valid.

severity.D10: FIXED — cross-dataset mismatch confirmed and corrected. Aligned binding shows Spearman r=-0.9, p=0.037 (significant). Level 1 binding is HIGHER with proper matching (0.740 vs 0.683), confirming per-problem coupling adds signal beyond population level. Original perfect r=-1.0 was coincidental.
severity.D2: FIXED — correctness labels realigned. AUROC jumped from 0.580 to 0.787 (+0.207). The mismatch was hiding a strong predictive signal. H0_persistence_entropy is top predictor.
severity.D6: moderate — Qwen on different problem set than Phi2/Pythia/StableLM. Aggregate H1 entropy comparison is likely robust. Aligned Qwen data now available for strict replication.

fix_applied.step1: DONE — Re-extracted Qwen hidden states from MATH-500 (scripts/extract_hidden_states_aligned.py --subset full). Output: math500_hidden_states_aligned.npz (500 problems, 43/90/105/128/134 per level, 18s, no skips).
fix_applied.step2: DONE — Re-ran D10 binding with aligned data. New scores in attention_binding_results_aligned.json. Trend preserved (r=-0.9, p=0.037).
fix_applied.step3: DONE — Re-ran D2 correctness prediction with aligned data. New AUROC=0.787 in correctness_prediction_results_aligned.json.
fix_applied.step4: PENDING — Re-run D6 cross-model with aligned Qwen data (optional, aggregate metrics likely robust).
